sections.paragraph:
χλευάζω (χλεύη) impf. ἐχλεύαζον; fut. 3 sg. χλευάσει (Pr 19:28 Aq Theod.; TestLevi 7:2); aor. 3 sg. ἐχλεύασε (Is 37:22 Sym.); aor. pass. impv. 3 pl. χλευασθήτωσαν (Pr 4:21 Theod.) (Aristoph., Demosth. et al.; late pap; TestLevi; Jos., Bell. 6, 365, C. Ap. 2, 137; Just., Tat.)

① to engage in mockery, mock, sneer, scoff (Philo, Sacr. Abel. 70; Jos., Ant. 7, 85; TestLevi 14:8) Ac 2:13 v.l. (for δια-); 17:32 (cp. Herm. Wr. 1, 29.—ASizoo, GereformTT 24, 1924, 289–97).

② to make fun of maliciously, mock, scoff at, sneer at, trans. τινά someone (so also Appian, Bell. Civ. 2, 153 §645; Lucian, Prom. in Verb. 33; LXX; TestLevi 7:2; Jos., Ant. 12, 170; Tat. 33, 2f; cp. Philo, Mos. 1, 29; Just., D. 137, 1 αὐτοῦ τοὺς μώλωπας; Tat. 22, 1 τὰς πανηγύρεις) 1 Cl 39:1. W. ὑβρίζειν (Plut., Artox. 1025 [27, 5]) Dg 2:7.—DELG s.v. χλεύη. New Docs 2, 104. M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

aor. aor. = aorist

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

impv. impv. = imperative

  1. pl. = plural

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

GereformTT GereformTT = Gereformeerd theologisch tijdschrift—List 6

trans. trans. = transitive

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1085
